The '''Temple employees''' were citizens of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] who were employed by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to maintain the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==History==
===Old Jedi Order===
The Temple was a vast complex that served as the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] main hub for training of students and boarding for knights. As such, it was in constant need of maintenance and upkeep. Within the [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]], offices were held where Jedi were assigned to hire and direct non-Jedi in cleaning and maintaining the Temple's large collection of artifacts under the authority of the [[Council of Reassignment/Legends|Council of Reassignment]]. Jedi would hire supervisors to watch and instruct these workers.<ref name="TCWCG" />

All jobs were divided into different groups. Maintenance, technicians, caretakers, and security, were some of the different factions. Maintenance workers were occupied with making sure that all doors, turbolifts, and droids were operational. A fleet of maintenance droids were kept within the Temple and were deployed to do basic checks and repairs. Technicians were employed mainly to focus on the electric, temperature, and lighting aspects of Temple life. There was also a subdivision focused on the upkeep of starfighters and transports with in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Temple's hangars]]. Caretakers were tasked with polishing statues, buffing marble, dusting holobooks, and other tasks to beautify the Temple. A group of droids were also on hand to assist with these tasks. Non-Forceful members of the [[Temple Security Force]] worked closely with the [[Jedi brute]]s and [[Jedi Sniper|snipers]] to defend the Temple from intruders.<ref name="TCWCG" />

Maintenance corridors ran along the main halls in order for workers to move about unseen. Undercoated, sparsely lit halls, with storage crates piled against the walls created a dismal and out of place appearance. Laundry chutes led to the automated laundry facilities that were placed throughout the Temple behind the hall's façade. Rumor among [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiates]] was it was impossible to descend through a chute, though they never claimed to know exactly how this was prevented.<ref name="LOTJB" />

During the final decades of the Republic, [[Lorn Pavan]] worked as a Jedi maintenance being until he was forced to resign when he attempted to make contact with his son [[Jax Pavan]], who was undergoing training in the [[Jedi academy|Temple's academy]].<ref name="DMSH">''[[Darth Maul: Shadow Hunter]]''</ref> Additionally, the [[Jinzler family]] was employed within the Temple halls, as their [[Lorana Jinzler|daughter]] was also a member of the Order.<ref name="OF">[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']]</ref> When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|public opinion]] turned on the Jedi and security on the Temple was increased. When the [[Bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar/Legends|Temple]] was bombed during the war, several mechanics were killed including demolitions expert [[Jackar Bowmani/Legends|Jackar Bowmani]].<ref name="TCWS">{{TCW|Sabotage (episode)}}</ref>

During [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]] at the close of the war, the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] entered the Temple and began to slaughter its occupants both Jedi and their aide-de-camps alike. Those employees who escaped went on to help in the underground organization known as [[Whiplash]], an anti-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] group bent on dismantling the Empire and restoring the Republic.<ref name="CNI" />

===New Jedi Order===
Several years after the fall of the Galactic Empire, the [[New Jedi Order]] once again employed citizens to maintain the [[new Jedi Temple]] and the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Ossus Academy]]. When both groups were evacuated during [[Darth Caedus]]'s reign, the caretakers fled with the Jedi to help maintain secrecy.
